answer:
I think one major thing schools should teach that they're not really focusing on now is financial literacy. I mean, when I look back at my education, we had math classes, but they didn't really dive into how to manage money or understand things like taxes, budgeting, or even credit scores. You know, these are skills that everyone will eventually need as adults, and it just seems crucial for people to learn this stuff early on.

For example, just recently I tried to open a bank account, and honestly, I was so confused about the different options available. If I had learned about basic banking in school, I would have felt much more confident making decisions like that. Also, understanding how to budget can empower students to make informed choices about their spending habits, which can lead to financial independence later in life.

Another aspect is the importance of investing and understanding how to grow wealth over time. With the rise of technology and apps that help manage finances, I think it's really relevant for students to get exposed to this. So many people end up struggling with debt or poor financial choices simply because they weren't educated about it. 

So, overall, I really believe integrating financial literacy into the curriculum could make a huge difference in students' lives, preparing them for real-world challenges and helping them become more financially savvy adults. It's something that would benefit everyone, not just those pursuing specific careers.
title: What should schools teach that they don't usually teach now? Why should all students learn this?
